pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 113: doc.save() writes to this path
Specified in task description and export_result.sh line 13
Web search confirms Courier is the industry standard screenplay font. Final Draft, Celtx, and professional screenwriting software use these fonts or equivalents
Web search: 12-point is the industry standard for screenplay formatting. Sources include Final Draft, Celtx, and NFI documentation confirming 12pt Courier as standard
Web search confirms left margin of 1.5 inches is industry standard for script binding. Final Draft and Celtx documentation confirm this
Web search confirms right margin of 1.0 inch is industry standard. Multiple sources confirm
Web search confirms top margin of 1.0 inch is industry standard
Web search confirms bottom margin of 1.0 inch is industry standard
setup_task.sh line 46: add_para("INT. OLD MAN'S BEDROOM - NIGHT")
setup_task.sh line 79: add_para("INT. OLD MAN'S BEDROOM - DAY")
setup_task.sh line 66: add_para("INT. HOUSE - HALLWAY - NIGHT")
Examined setup_task.sh lines 41-111 which contains all add_para() calls creating screenplay content. This scene heading does not appear anywhere in the script
setup_task.sh line 43: add_para("FADE IN:")
setup_task.sh line 63 and 76: add_para("CUT TO:") appears twice
setup_task.sh line 111: add_para("FADE OUT.")
setup_task.sh lines 52 and 59: add_para("NARRATOR (V.O.)")

Examined setup_task.sh lines 41-111. 'OLD MAN' appears only in stage direction text (e.g., line 49: 'sleeping OLD MAN's face' and line 90: 'The old man, I mentioned') but NEVER as a dialogue heading/character name. Character names are standalone add_para() calls. Only NARRATOR, OFFICER DUPIN, and OFFICER 2 are actual character name lines
setup_task.sh lines 72, 89, 100, 107: add_para("NARRATOR")
setup_task.sh line 85: add_para("OFFICER DUPIN")
setup_task.sh line 96: add_para("OFFICER 2")

privileged_info_summary: The screenplay document is a Tell-Tale Heart adaptation with 3 scene headings (INT. OLD MAN'S BEDROOM - NIGHT, INT. HOUSE - HALLWAY - NIGHT, INT. OLD MAN'S BEDROOM - DAY), 3 transitions (FADE IN:, CUT TO:, FADE OUT.), and 4 unique character names (NARRATOR (V.O.), NARRATOR, OFFICER DUPIN, OFFICER 2). Industry standard formatting requires: Courier or Liberation Mono font at 12 points, left margin 1.5 inches, right/top/bottom margins 1.0 inch. Initial document uses wrong font (Liberation Serif 11pt) and wrong margins (1.0 inch all around).
